tldr: Almost every one of your 19 live ads uses the exact same body copy and the same 'Mid-Season Sale Ends Soon' headline. Meta treats these as basically one ad, so most of them are being shown to almost no one while you keep paying to run them.

benchmark.context: The typical sportswear brand ships a fresh ad every three weeks, and the best ones every two. You're only running four genuinely different ad ideas across 19 ads, and Meta needs at least five before it stops quietly hiding the near-copies.

ads.headline: Mid-Season Sale Ends Soon.
ads.body: For those that train with intent. Every session matters, your gear should too. Discover performance sportswear designed to move with precision, built for those who show up with purpose.
drivers: The exact same body copy is copied word-for-word across roughly 17 of your live ads.
Meta reads all these near-identical ads as one ad, so most of your 19 are barely being shown.
The headline promises a sale but never says what percent off, so shoppers have no reason to click today.

ads.headline: Mid-Season Sale Ends Soon.
ads.body: For those that train with intent. Every session matters, your gear should too. Discover performance sportswear designed to move with precision, built for those who show up with purpose.
drivers: This ad is a near-twin of another one running right now with the same headline, body, and button.
The opening line 'For those that train with intent' shows up on nearly every video you're running.
Sends people to your full store instead of a specific product, so they arrive with no clear next step.

ads.headline: Mid-Season Sale Ends Soon.
ads.body: For those that train with intent. Every session matters, your gear should too. Discover performance sportswear designed to move with precision, built for those who show up with purpose.
drivers: Another word-for-word copy of your most-run ad, competing against itself in the same auctions.
Zero of your 19 ads use a single product image, so the algorithm has no cheaper still to lean on.
The body reads like a brand mission statement, not a reason to buy this week.

ads.headline: Mid-Season Sale Ends Soon.
ads.body: For those that train with intent. Every session matters, your gear should too. Discover performance sportswear designed to move with precision, built for those who show up with purpose.
drivers: You have 13 videos all opening with the same script, so the ones running longest are the only ones being shown.
No specific product, price, or fabric name anywhere in the copy for a shopper to hold onto.
'Sale ends soon' has no date, so the urgency feels empty after seven days of it running.

ads.headline: Mid-Season Sale Ends Soon.
ads.body: For those that train with intent. Every session matters, your gear should too. Discover performance sportswear designed to move with precision, built for those who show up with purpose.
drivers: Same headline and body as your 'Shop now' twin, only the button changed - Meta still reads them as one ad.
'Get offer' as a button hints at a discount code that the ad never actually shows.
You're splitting your budget between duplicates instead of behind genuinely different ideas.

hero_concept.headline: Built for the tenth rep.
hero_concept.primary_text:
Most training tops feel fine on rep one. By rep ten, they're a wet flag stuck to your back.

The AeroLite Short Sleeve was cut for the second half of the session - laser-perforated across the shoulders, hem that stays put through overhead work, weight so light you forget you're wearing it.

Mid-Season Sale on now. Free UK delivery over £75.
hero_concept.visual_direction: A single athlete mid-set in a low-lit gym, sweat catching the light on the AeroLite Short Sleeve, camera close enough to read the fabric texture.
hero_concept.fills_gap: Every one of your 19 ads talks in broad brand language and never names a single product. This ad idea flips that: one shirt, one specific moment, one clear reason to buy - the kind of ad that gives Meta something genuinely different to show instead of another copy of the same script.
hero_concept.image_prompt: A photorealistic editorial product photograph featuring the product from the reference image, styled in a low-lit concrete-and-steel training gym with a single athlete captured mid-set, sweat visible on the fabric across the shoulders and back, with cinematic side-lighting from a cool north-facing window and a soft rim light picking out the shirt's texture and perforations. Moody, high-contrast, editorial sportswear aesthetic in the vein of a premium performance-apparel campaign. Square 1:1 composition with breathing room top and bottom for ad text overlay. CRITICAL - preserve the exact colorway, fabric weight, and technical knit finish of the AeroLite Short Sleeve as shown in the reference image, including any laser-cut perforation zones, tonal stitching, and matte performance-fabric sheen - strictly NO color shift, NO swapping to a different shade, NO glossy or shiny fabric treatment, NO softening of the technical fabric to a cotton look, NO warm-tone bias. STRUCTURE - preserve the exact construction of the AeroLite Short Sleeve: crew neckline, short sleeves at the exact length shown in the reference, straight athletic-fit body, hem length as shown, any perforation panels, seam placements, and small brand label positioned exactly where shown on the reference. Do NOT add or remove seams, panels, perforation zones, pockets, zippers, or hem details, do NOT change the neckline shape or sleeve length, do NOT alter the fit or silhouette, do NOT relocate or remove the brand label. Render the product's metal, color, finish, fabric, weave, stones, surface treatment, AND every visible construction detail (silhouette, closures, hardware count, pockets, sleeves, neckline, printed graphics) EXACTLY as they appear in the reference image. Do NOT change the metal type, do NOT shift the color temperature, do NOT introduce tones not present in the reference, do NOT add or remove buttons, snaps, zippers, pockets, straps, or any hardware, do NOT alter the silhouette, do NOT remove or modify printed graphics or text on the garment. IMPORTANT: the 'no text' and 'no logos' items in the NEGATIVE list below apply ONLY to text or logos added as overlay to the photograph itself - text, logos, graphics, prints, and brand labels that exist ON the product in the reference image are part of the product and must be preserved exactly. Premium commerce photography quality. NEGATIVE: no overlay text added to the photograph, no overlay logos added to the photograph, no watermarks, no UI elements, no Facebook interface, no clickable buttons, no faces, no models, no collage, no borders, no duplicate products, no color shift, no metal swap, no silver-to-gold or gold-to-silver conversion, no warm-tone bias on cool metals, no recoloring of fabric or stones, no pattern modifications, no removing or replacing the product's printed graphics, no extra hardware not present in reference, no added pockets or pleats, no silhouette alterations, no design modifications.

next_step.urgency: Roughly a dozen of your ads look identical to Meta, and most of them stop getting shown within the next week.
next_step.headline: Walk through the Mathan refresh plan with Kyle
next_step.body:
Matthew, in this 30 min call, we'll map out:
• 3 customer avatars at your price point
• The motivational hook driving each
• A 30-day creative + spend roadmap (to outbeat your current ROAS)

Yours to keep - work with us or don't.

method_note: Based only on your public Facebook and Instagram ads - the pictures, the words, and how long each one has been running. No access to your account or private numbers.
